name: Ornl
description_short: ORNL Federal Credit Union is a Tennessee-based credit union established in 1948, serving over 202,600 members with $3.8B in assets and comprehensive banking services.
description_long:
ORNL Federal Credit Union was established in 1948 and is headquartered in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, making it the 4th largest credit union in the state. The institution has grown to serve over 202,600 members and stewards more than $3.8 billion in assets, supported by over 500 dedicated employees. Its primary mission centers on providing essential banking services to a specific geographic community, enhancing financial accessibility for local residents and businesses in the Oak Ridge area.

ORNL offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and IRA/KEOGH accounts. Members can access wire transfers, bill pay, online loan payments, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. The credit union also provides financial counseling, financial education programs, financial workshops, and student scholarships. Additional conveniences include a drive-thru teller service and electronic signature authentication for digital banking needs.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its cooperative structure and member-focused approach, offering 29 ATMs across its network and maintaining a single main branch location at 215 S Rutgers Avenue in Oak Ridge. ORNL provides membership eligibility screening to serve its specific geographic community, with account offerings across regular savings (254,400+ accounts), share drafts (147,500+ accounts), money market accounts (9,400+), and share certificates/CDs (27,300+). The institution supports financial literacy through educational workshops and student scholarships, demonstrating community investment beyond basic banking services.

However, recent member reviews reveal significant service quality concerns. The Main Office location carries a 2.1/5 member rating based on 19 reviews, with documented complaints about unresolved lien payoff issues, technical problems persisting for weeks, slow document turnaround times (1-2 weeks for account closure letters), and declining service quality over recent years. Multiple long-term members report deterioration in service compared to historical performance. These operational issues present a meaningful caveat for prospective members despite the institution's solid financial foundation and comprehensive product offerings.
services: Bill pay and online loan payments
Mobile payments and remote deposit capture
pros: 4th largest credit union in Tennessee with $3.8B in assets and strong financial stability
Extensive product suite including personal loans, auto loans, business loans, credit cards, and investment accounts
29 ATMs providing convenient cash access beyond the main branch
Comprehensive financial education programs, workshops, and student scholarship offerings
Online banking, mobile app, and modern digital services including remote deposit capture
Over 202,600 members and 500+ employees indicating established operational scale
Member-focused cooperative structure serving specific geographic community
cons: Main Office location rated only 2.1/5 stars based on recent member feedback indicating service quality concerns
Single branch location limits in-person banking convenience for members outside Oak Ridge area
Documented operational issues including unresolved lien payoffs, persistent technical problems, and slow document processing
Multiple long-term members report service deterioration in recent years compared to historical performance
Lobby currently closed with limited clarity on branch hours and availability
best_for: Oak Ridge, Tennessee residents and businesses seeking comprehensive credit union banking with local roots
Members prioritizing financial education, workshops, and student scholarship programs
Borrowers seeking business loans and commercial lending products from a cooperative institution
Long-established members with historical positive relationships who may experience better service than new members
diagnosis: ORNL Federal Credit Union is best suited for Oak Ridge-area residents and businesses seeking a stable, member-owned financial institution with comprehensive lending and deposit products. Primary caveat: recent member ratings (2.1/5 stars) document significant operational concerns including unresolved account issues, technical failures, and slow service resolution—prospective members should weigh the institution's solid financial foundation against documented service quality deterioration.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
